title: Restart macOS gxserver when bundled server build identity changes
description: Second-round High finding from SECOND_ROUND_SERVER_REVIEW.md. GxserverClient.startOrReuse accepts any authenticated same-protocol daemon with required tools, so an app update can keep talking to an old running gxserver and miss server-side fixes. Add a gxserver package/build fingerprint to health/runtime metadata and compare it against the bundled expected value, or otherwise force restart on same-protocol package mismatch.
acceptance_criteria: Installing/running a newer app bundle while an older gxserver daemon is alive causes the macOS host to stop/restart the old daemon before sidebar hydration; same-build daemon reuse still works; tests or deterministic proof cover both match and mismatch cases.

comments.author: madda
comments.text: Claimed for worker: macOS gxserver build identity and daemon restart behavior.

comments.author: madda
comments.text: Added gxserver build identity metadata to authenticated health/runtime state, generated deterministic package fingerprints during packaging, and made the macOS launcher restart mismatched same-protocol daemons before sidebar hydration while preserving same-build reuse.

title: Avoid shell argv limits for zmx send payloads
description: Second-round Medium finding from SECOND_ROUND_SERVER_REVIEW.md. sendSessionText/sendSessionMessage accept body payloads up to the JSON limit but embed text into a /bin/zsh -lc script argument. Payloads below the HTTP cap can still exceed OS argv limits or fail at spawn time. Add explicit text-size limits and/or pass payloads through stdin/temp file/direct zmx invocation.
acceptance_criteria: zmx send payload handling has deliberate size limits and no longer fails accidentally from shell argv length for valid requests; oversized sends return clear client errors; tests cover large payload rejection or stdin/direct path behavior.

comments.author: madda
comments.text: Claimed for worker: zmx send payload size/argv handling.

comments.author: madda
comments.text: Implemented stdin-backed zmx send handling with an explicit send payload byte limit, so valid send requests are not placed in the shell argv script and oversized payloads return clear client errors; added endpoint and runner regressions.

title: Make legacy migration crash-idempotent before completion marker
description: High migration deploy risk. If gxserver exits after inserting DB rows but before recording migration metadata, next launch treats import as notRun and imports duplicate P/G projects/sessions. Add idempotent lookup/upsert by legacyProjectId/legacySessionId or record resumable progress safely.
acceptance_criteria: Injected crash after DB import and before marker does not duplicate projects or sessions on rerun; test verifies stable canonical IDs and single rows per legacy identity.

comments.author: madda
comments.text: Claimed for worker: make first-run legacy migration crash-idempotent before the completion marker is written.

comments.author: madda
comments.text: Started crash-idempotency fix for legacy migration reruns after partial SQLite import.

comments.author: madda
comments.text: Made legacy macOS state import retry-safe before the completion marker by reusing existing legacy project/session metadata and added a crash-rerun regression test. gxserver check passes.

title: Classify zmx list/probe failures as unknown, not missing
description: High server deploy risk from SERVER_REVIEW_NOTES.md. probeZmxSession uses a shell pipeline where zmx list failure with no output is indistinguishable from grep miss, causing missing classification and bad attach/startup decisions.
acceptance_criteria: Fake zmx list failure returns provider lifecycle unknown with logged failure, while real absent session still returns missing; tests cover both.

comments.author: madda
comments.text: Claimed for worker: make zmx list/probe execution failures produce unknown lifecycle state instead of missing.

comments.author: madda
comments.text: Changed zmx provider probing so command failures, thrown runner errors, and probe timeouts report unknown with error details, while successful zmx list responses that lack the session still report missing. Added regression coverage for failed list vs successful absence.

title: Use Swift-equivalent freshness when gxserver reads legacy shared vs WK storage
description: Medium migration risk. gxserver readLegacyStateSnapshot always prefers shared JSON when present, while Swift scores WK localStorage vs shared file. Direct gxserver start or startup ordering can migrate stale shared files and mark import complete.
acceptance_criteria: Migration chooses the richer/fresher projects and previous-sessions snapshot consistently with GhostexAppStorage scoring; tests cover stale shared file plus richer WK/localStorage source.

comments.author: madda
comments.text: Claimed for worker: make gxserver legacy migration choose shared vs WK/localStorage using Swift-equivalent freshness/richness scoring.

comments.author: madda
comments.text: Implemented Swift-equivalent legacy storage selection in gxserver migration: canonical shared P/G snapshots remain authoritative, otherwise projects and previous sessions choose the richer scored WK/localStorage payload over stale shared JSON. Added regression coverage for stale shared files with richer localStorage winning while preserving the shared-file backup path.

title: Implement or remove gx CLI session interaction endpoints
description: High server deploy risk. API catalog and gx CLI route focus/read/send session commands to gxserver endpoints that dispatch as notImplemented. Either implement focus/read/send APIs or stop advertising/routing them before cutover.
acceptance_criteria: gx focus/read-text/send-text/send-enter/send-message work through gxserver with tests, or CLI/API visibly reject unsupported commands without pretending support.

comments.author: madda
comments.text: Claimed for worker: make gx CLI focus/read/send session interaction commands work through gxserver or clearly remove unsupported routing before cutover.

comments.author: madda
comments.text: Implemented gxserver-backed zmx session read/send/send-enter and session-targeted send-message paths, with explicit unsupported responses for renderer focus and agent-targeted visible-session creation until gxserver has a renderer event channel. Added focused API and helper coverage plus CLI help messaging.

title: Make legacy log import crash-idempotent
description: Second-round Low finding from SECOND_ROUND_MIGRATION_REVIEW.md. Legacy logs are appended to gxserver.jsonl before the SQLite migration marker is recorded, so a crash after log append but before marker can append duplicate migrated log lines on next launch. This does not corrupt project/session state, but can make upgrade diagnostics noisy. Add idempotency keys/checkpoints or explicitly document and test acceptable duplicate behavior.
acceptance_criteria: Crash after legacy log append and before migration marker does not duplicate imported log lines on rerun, or duplicate behavior is deliberately documented and tested as acceptable; project/session migration idempotency remains intact.

comments.author: madda
comments.text: Captured the remaining second-round Low migration finding for later: log import crash idempotency; not part of this high/medium fix pass.

title: Make WK localStorage database selection deterministic and freshness-aware
description: Medium migration risk. findLocalStorageDatabases reads every localstorage.sqlite3 and readLegacyLocalStorageValues takes first value by enumeration order. Multiple WebKit DBs can import stale settings/agents/project commands.
acceptance_criteria: When multiple localStorage DBs contain Ghostex keys, migration selects the newest/highest-scoring value deterministically; tests cover stale-first enumeration.

comments.author: madda
comments.text: Claimed for worker: make WK localStorage database/value selection deterministic and freshness-aware when multiple WebKit stores contain Ghostex keys.

comments.author: madda
comments.text: Implemented deterministic WK localStorage candidate ranking for gxserver migration and native bootstrap. Multiple WebKit databases now prefer canonical/richer sidebar payloads, then fresher database mtimes, then stable path tie-breaks; regression coverage creates stale-first SQLite stores and verifies the richer newer projects and previous sessions are imported.

title: Add timeout and output caps to typed subprocess operations
description: High server deploy risk. runGitAction/runWorktreeAction/runBeadsAction buffer stdout/stderr without timeout or size cap, allowing long hangs or unbounded memory growth.
acceptance_criteria: Typed operations enforce timeout, stdout/stderr byte caps, and terminate child process on overrun/client abort where possible; tests cover timeout and output truncation/failure behavior.

comments.author: madda
comments.text: Claimed for worker: add timeout and output caps to typed subprocess operations so git/worktree/beads actions cannot hang or buffer unbounded output.

comments.author: madda
comments.text: Typed Git/worktree/Beads subprocesses now enforce bounded runtime and stdout/stderr buffers, terminate on timeout/output cap/client abort where possible, and return structured operation errors for timeout and output overruns. Added focused tests for timeout plus stdout and stderr cap behavior.

title: Keep gxserver local listener fixed or fail invalid config
description: Second-round Medium finding from SECOND_ROUND_SERVER_REVIEW.md. Config merging allows listeners.local.host/port to override the fixed local API listener, while runtime metadata, status checks, CLI, docs, and macOS/sidebar clients assume 127.0.0.1:58744. Make local host/port immutable or fail startup with a clear validation error if configured.
acceptance_criteria: Config cannot silently move the local listener away from 127.0.0.1:58744; startup either ignores/disallows those fields consistently; tests cover attempted local host/port override and default behavior.

comments.author: madda
comments.text: Claimed for worker: fixed local listener config contract while zmx server work continues separately.

comments.author: madda
comments.text: Fixed local listener config validation so gxserver startup fails clearly when config.json attempts to move the local API away from 127.0.0.1:58744, while remote listener host and port remain configurable. Added storage/API regression coverage for fixed defaults, rejected local overrides, and the remote-bind cleanup fixture under the fixed local listener contract.

title: Restrict CORS and private-network headers to trusted gxserver clients
description: Medium server risk. applyCorsHeaders returns Access-Control-Allow-Origin: * and Access-Control-Allow-Private-Network: true for all listeners. Auth protects APIs, but browser attack surface is broader than needed.
acceptance_criteria: CORS/PNA policy is limited to native/sidebar development origins or explicit allowed config, preserving WK sidebar functionality; tests cover allowed and disallowed origins.

comments.author: madda
comments.text: Claimed for worker: restrict gxserver CORS and private-network headers to trusted client origins instead of wildcard exposure.

comments.author: madda
comments.text: Restricted gxserver CORS and private-network response headers to native WK/sidebar development origins or exact config allowlist entries, while keeping non-browser CLI/server requests free of CORS headers. Added coverage for trusted origins, disallowed origins, no-origin clients, configured origins, and private-network preflights.

title: Surface corrupted SQLite JSON columns instead of silently emptying state
description: Low server risk. domain-state JSON parse helpers return {} or [] on corruption, which can hide persistence corruption and allow later updates to overwrite data with empty state.
acceptance_criteria: Corrupt JSON columns produce explicit degraded/corrupt state warnings or errors and avoid silent destructive overwrite; tests cover corrupt project/session JSON read/update behavior.

comments.author: madda
comments.text: Claimed for worker: surface corrupted SQLite JSON columns instead of silently emptying state and risking destructive overwrite.

comments.author: madda
comments.text: Corrupt project/session SQLite JSON columns now surface explicit corruptState errors on reads and updates instead of normalizing to empty state; update paths fail before writing so recoverable persisted rows are not overwritten. Added repository and API coverage for malformed project JSON and wrongly shaped session JSON.

title: Define and enforce settings freshness source policy during migration
description: Second-round Medium finding from SECOND_ROUND_MIGRATION_REVIEW.md. Settings import currently prefers any native-sidebar-settings.json over WK ghostex-native-settings with no freshness/richness scoring. Decide the intended source of truth and implement deterministic behavior so stale shared settings cannot silently override newer WK settings unless that is explicitly correct.
acceptance_criteria: Settings migration has an explicit shared-vs-WK source policy with regression coverage for disagreement; if WK can be newer, migration chooses it deterministically; if shared is authoritative, tests and comments document why.

comments.author: madda
comments.text: Claimed for worker: deterministic settings source policy during legacy migration.

comments.author: madda
comments.text: Defined the gxserver settings migration policy to match current macOS behavior: an existing shared settings file is authoritative, while WK settings only seed missing shared settings. Added a regression where richer conflicting WK settings cannot override shared terminal, notification, and Auto Sleep settings during migration.

title: Do not mark zmx provider missing when kill fails
description: High server deploy risk. sleepSession/killSession persists sleeping/stopped plus provider missing even when zmx kill failed. This can lose route to live sessions.
acceptance_criteria: When killZmxSession fails, persisted session/provider state remains consistent with possible live provider and response/log communicates failure; tests cover sleep and kill failure.

comments.author: madda
comments.text: Claimed for worker: preserve possible-live zmx provider state when sleep or kill operations fail instead of persisting missing/stopped state.

comments.author: madda
comments.text: Implemented kill failure handling so sleepSession and killSession persist unknown session/provider lifecycle with kill error details instead of sleeping/stopped plus provider missing. Added regressions for both endpoints and verified the gxserver build plus focused lifecycle/API tests.

title: Make SSH gxserver profiles establish and manage tunnels before RPC
description: High remote deploy risk. CLI resolves SSH forwardPlan but requestGxserverRpc fetches baseUrl directly; default local forward port can conflict with local gxserver. Implement tunnel check/start or clear user-facing failure guidance.
acceptance_criteria: SSH profile command starts/checks port forward before RPC, avoids local port conflict, and tests cover no-existing-forward and occupied local gxserver port.

comments.author: madda
comments.text: Claimed for worker: make SSH gxserver profiles establish/manage a tunnel before RPC and avoid conflicting with the local gxserver port.

comments.author: madda
comments.text: Implemented SSH profile tunnel setup before gxserver RPC: the CLI now checks/starts remote gxserver over SSH, chooses a non-local-daemon loopback port by default, verifies tunnel health before fetching, tears down CLI-owned tunnel children after request bursts, and reports SSH/setup failures with direct guidance. Added focused CLI tests for no-existing-forward and occupied local gxserver port behavior.

comments.author: madda
comments.text: Fixed the gxserver remote-client regression test for the SSH tunnel arg change: the expected port-forward command now includes ExitOnForwardFailure=yes, preserving the fail-fast tunnel requirement while matching the helper output. Verified with gxserver build, focused compiled remote-client test, CLI SSH test file, and diff check.

title: Avoid exposing credential tokens through process argv
description: Low server risk. credential-store writes use security add-generic-password -w <secret>, and CLI supports one-shot --token. Local process listing/history can expose bearer tokens.
acceptance_criteria: Credential writes pass secrets through stdin or safer mechanism where possible; CLI avoids encouraging argv token use or warns clearly; tests/mocks cover command construction.

comments.author: madda
comments.text: Claimed for worker: avoid exposing credential tokens through process argv and clarify safer CLI token guidance.

comments.author: madda
comments.text: Credential-store writes now avoid bearer tokens in process argv by feeding macOS security via stdin/prompt mode; gx remote token guidance now prefers OS credential refs or --token-stdin, with legacy --token clearly marked as argv-exposing. Focused tests cover command construction and CLI guidance.

title: Clean up local listener if remote listener startup fails
description: Medium server risk. runGxserverForeground starts local listener then remote listener; if remote bind fails, local listener may remain half-started without runtime metadata.
acceptance_criteria: Remote listener bind failure closes any started local server and exits cleanly; status does not see a half-started daemon; test covers occupied remote port.

comments.author: madda
comments.text: Claimed for worker: close any started local listener if remote listener startup fails so gxserver does not remain half-started.

comments.author: madda
comments.text: Implemented all-or-nothing foreground listener startup: if the remote listener bind fails after local startup, gxserver closes the local listener, removes runtime metadata, and exits cleanly. Added a real CLI regression with an occupied remote port that verifies the local listener is released and status remains stopped.

title: Bound domain-state JSON payload size and depth
description: Medium server risk. Project/session APIs accept unbounded nested JSON into SQLite, making durable oversized rows and huge list responses possible.
acceptance_criteria: Create/update project/session reject or truncate oversized/deep JSON consistently with migration limits; tests cover runtimeSettings/previousSessionHistory oversized payloads.

comments.author: madda
comments.text: Claimed for worker: bound domain-state JSON payload size/depth so project/session APIs cannot persist oversized nested SQLite rows.

comments.author: madda
comments.text: Added live project/session domain-state JSON bounds before SQLite persistence: shared JSON columns now reject payloads over the migration-sized envelope or deeper than migration depth instead of truncating user state. Added API coverage for oversized runtime settings and too-deep previous-session/session runtime JSON, with rejected writes verified not to persist.

title: Canonicalize previous-session history stored in gxserver DB
description: Medium migration risk. previousSessionHistoryJson is stored from legacy previous sessions without recursive ID remap, unlike shared previous-sessions JSON. gxserver clients may receive legacy project/session IDs.
acceptance_criteria: Migration stores canonical project/session IDs in previousSessionHistoryJson except explicit hidden legacy restore metadata; tests assert nested sessionRecord/sessionId/projectId remapping.

comments.author: madda
comments.text: Claimed for worker: canonicalize previous-session history stored in gxserver DB so clients do not receive legacy project/session IDs after migration.

comments.author: madda
comments.text: Canonicalized migrated previous-session history stored in gxserver project rows by reserving canonical G IDs for legacy history records, recursively remapping visible project/session references, and preserving legacy IDs only under hidden restore metadata. Added first-run and completed-import repair coverage for DB/listProjects history output.

title: Bound gxserver readSessionText zmx history output
description: Second-round High finding from SECOND_ROUND_SERVER_REVIEW.md. /api/readSessionText returns full zmx history output and runZshScript accumulates stdout/stderr without caps, so authenticated clients can force large memory/JSON responses from long-running sessions. Add byte/line limits, truncation metadata, pagination, streaming, or equivalent bounded behavior before stdout accumulation becomes unbounded.
acceptance_criteria: readSessionText cannot buffer or return unbounded zmx history; oversized history responses are capped or paginated with explicit metadata/errors; tests cover large history output and normal reads.

comments.author: madda
comments.text: Claimed for worker: zmx readSessionText bounded history output.

comments.author: madda
comments.text: Implemented bounded readSessionText history handling by capping zmx history stdout and returning explicit truncation metadata when the cap is reached; added focused API and runner regressions for large history output and normal reads.

title: Bound Beads board reads in gxserver typed operations
description: Second-round Medium finding from SECOND_ROUND_SERVER_REVIEW.md. runBeadsAction board bypasses bounded subprocess handling and reads/parses the entire .beads/issues.jsonl file into memory. Add file-size, row-count, and/or response-size bounds with truncation metadata or explicit typed errors.
acceptance_criteria: Beads board action cannot load unbounded issues.jsonl into memory; oversized board data returns a clear bounded response or error; tests cover normal board reads and oversized board state.

comments.author: madda
comments.text: Claimed for worker: bound Beads board reads without touching launch/build identity work.

comments.author: madda
comments.text: Bound Beads board reads by checking issues.jsonl size before streaming, enforcing row and serialized-response limits while parsing, and returning typed bad-request errors for oversized board state. Normal board reads keep the existing issues/stdout response shape, with focused coverage for normal and oversized cases.

title: Remap project-board bead conversation links in gxserver DB import
description: High migration deploy risk. shared sidebar rewrite remaps ghostexSessionId/sessionId/projectId, but projectBoardConfigJson imports beadConversationLinks directly with legacy g-* IDs. Remap links before storing projectBoardConfig in SQLite.
acceptance_criteria: Migration fixture with beadConversationLinks containing legacy project/session IDs stores canonical P/G IDs in projects.projectBoardConfigJson and listProjects response.

comments.author: madda
comments.text: Claimed for worker: remap project-board bead conversation links from legacy IDs to canonical gxserver project/session IDs during DB import.

comments.author: madda
comments.text: Migration now remaps project-board bead conversation link project/session references to canonical P/G IDs during DB import, repairs stale completed-import configs, and covers DB/listProjects plus rewritten shared sidebar state.

title: Block sidebar hydration until gxserver migration is canonical
description: Critical migration deploy risk from MIGRATION_REVIEW_NOTES.md. On first upgraded launch, AppDelegate can create the window/sidebar before gxserver migration rewrites shared state. React can hydrate stale project-*/g-* IDs and later write them back over canonical P/G files. Ensure migration completes before sidebar hydration or force a canonical state reload before any sidebar persistence.
acceptance_criteria: Clean legacy profile first launch never renders or persists project-*/g-* IDs after gxserver migration; WK/localStorage is updated to canonical data before sidebar writes; regression test or integration proof covers first-launch ordering.

comments.author: madda
comments.text: Claimed for worker: fix first-launch startup ordering so sidebar cannot hydrate/persist stale legacy IDs before gxserver migration is canonical.

comments.author: madda
comments.text: Delayed macOS sidebar window creation until gxserver bootstrap completes so first upgraded launch reads the post-migration shared sidebar state. Added a React-side persistence guard and focused storage tests so a stale legacy project/session tree cannot overwrite canonical gxserver P/G storage if it ever reaches the client.

title: Make log querying bounded for large JSONL files
description: Medium server risk. queryLogs reads, filters, sorts the whole gxserver.jsonl before applying limit. Long-running installs can spike memory/CPU.
acceptance_criteria: queryLogs scales with requested limit/tail window where possible, has file-size safeguards, and tests cover large log file behavior.

comments.author: madda
comments.text: Claimed for worker: make JSONL log querying bounded so large gxserver logs do not require full-file read/filter/sort before applying limits.

comments.author: madda
comments.text: Implemented bounded gxserver JSONL log querying: small files still get exact scans, while large files read a bounded head or tail window sized from the requested limit and expose metadata when counts only cover the scanned window. Added a large-log test covering bounded tail reads and preserved exact metadata for small filtered queries.

title: Enforce HTTP request body size limits
description: Medium server risk. readRequestJson buffers authenticated POST bodies without a size limit. Remote authenticated clients can force memory growth.
acceptance_criteria: Server rejects oversized JSON bodies with explicit error before unbounded buffering; tests cover limit and normal requests.

comments.author: madda
comments.text: Claimed for worker: enforce HTTP request body size limits so authenticated POST bodies cannot be buffered without bound.

comments.author: madda
comments.text: Added a 1 MiB gxserver JSON RPC body limit that rejects oversized Content-Length values before reading and stops buffering chunked bodies once the limit is crossed; added API coverage for normal and oversized authenticated POST bodies.

title: Remap or exempt legacy browser and T3 pane IDs during gxserver migration
description: Second-round High finding from SECOND_ROUND_MIGRATION_REVIEW.md. Migrated shared project snapshots can keep legacy g-* IDs for non-terminal browser/T3 panes. Because the rewritten shared file is marked gxserverMigratedAt, React treats it as canonical and can block future persistence when outgoing payloads still contain those IDs. Decide and implement the correct policy: either allocate/remap every session-bearing pane to canonical G IDs, or narrow legacy-ID detection/remapping to daemon-owned terminal IDs only if browser/T3 IDs intentionally remain client-local.
acceptance_criteria: Upgrade fixture with browser and/or T3 pane IDs no longer blocks shared project persistence after migration; regression tests cover the selected policy; existing terminal/session ID migration behavior stays intact.

comments.author: madda
comments.text: Claimed for worker: migration/sidebar policy for legacy non-terminal pane IDs.

comments.author: madda
comments.text: Implemented the client-local policy for browser and T3 panes: gxserver migration keeps their legacy-shaped pane IDs while daemon-owned terminal IDs continue to canonicalize to G IDs. Updated the sidebar persistence guard so migrated browser/T3 IDs no longer block shared project persistence, with regressions covering both allowed client-local panes and rejected terminal IDs.

title: Canonicalize empty previous-sessions shared state during migration
description: Low migration risk. rewriteSharedSidebarStateWithGxserverIds skips writing previous-sessions file when snapshot is empty, and Swift does not consider empty arrays migrated. Old WK previous-session history may reappear.
acceptance_criteria: Migration writes an authoritative empty previous-sessions state or marker; Swift treats it as migrated; test covers shared [] plus stale WK previous sessions.

comments.author: madda
comments.text: Claimed for worker: make empty previous-sessions shared state authoritative during migration so stale WK history cannot reappear.

comments.author: madda
comments.text: Claimed the migration fix; inspecting shared previous-session rewrite and migrated-state detection before patching.

comments.author: madda
comments.text: Made empty previous-session migration state authoritative by always rewriting the shared previous-sessions file, treating shared [] as migrated when the adjacent projects snapshot is migrated, and adding regression coverage for stale WK history not reappearing during completed repair.

title: Include command-panel sessions in migration freshness scoring
description: Second-round Medium finding from SECOND_ROUND_MIGRATION_REVIEW.md. Shared-vs-WK projectSnapshotScore only counts workspace grid sessions, while importer also migrates commandsPanel.sessions. A richer WK payload can lose to stale shared JSON if the only newer state is command-panel sessions or active command references.
acceptance_criteria: Project freshness scoring accounts for commandsPanel.sessions and relevant active command-panel state; regression test proves WK wins when it is richer only through command-panel sessions; existing shared-vs-WK migration tests still pass.

comments.author: madda
comments.text: Claimed for worker: project freshness scoring for command-panel sessions.

comments.author: madda
comments.text: Updated migration freshness scoring to count command-panel sessions and active command-panel session references as migration-relevant state, with structural project/session data ranked above unrelated JSON byte size. Added a regression where WK localStorage wins over a larger stale shared payload solely because it contains the command-panel terminal.
